202 ENGX - English Education Abroad

ENGX405 Hans Christian Andersen and the Danish Golden Age (3 Credits) Hans Christian Andersen (1805-1875) is internationally known as the writer of fairy tales. Children all over the world know The Ugly Duckling, The Emperor's New Clothes, The Little Mermaid, The Princess and the Pea and other tales. But Andersen also wrote plays, novels, poems, travelogues and songs. This course will be a study of approximately 30 fairy tales by Hans Christian Andersen (1805-75) as well as extracts from his travelogues, poems, diaries and his autobiography, The Fairy Tale of My Life.

Additional Information: This course is offered as part of the Maryland- in-Copenhagen study abroad program. More information at ter.ps/ UMCopenhagen.
